whoWeAreSection.body:
The Infectious Diseases Institute (IDI), Makerere University's College of Computing & Health Sciences in partnership with the US Government National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases and the Office of Cyber Infrastructure and Computational Biology (NIH/NIAID/OCICB) have established the African Center of Excellence in Bioinformatics & Data Sciences — one of only two such centres on the African continent.

First of its kind on the Makerere campus, the ACE is a centre for Computational Biology and Big Data analysis. It houses a dedicated High-Performance Computing cluster, a Tele-learning Centre, collaborative rooms, and a Virtual Reality room for the latest 3D & VR pedagogical and diagnostic approaches. Layered over those facilities are professionally curated long-term (MSc & PhD) and short-term (Certificate) academic programmes, as well as research and development support units.

introSection.heading: About ACE
introSection.description: The African Center of Excellence in Bioinformatics and Data-Intensive Sciences is dedicated to advancing computational research, building capacity, and fostering innovation across Africa. Established to address the growing need for expertise in bioinformatics, data science, and high-performance computing, we train world-class researchers and tackle critical challenges in health, agriculture, and climate.

missionVision.mission: To advance computational research and capacity building in bioinformatics, data science, and high-performance computing across Africa, fostering innovation and addressing critical challenges in health, agriculture, and climate.
missionVision.vision: To be a leading center of excellence in computational sciences, driving transformative research and producing world-class graduates who contribute to sustainable development in Africa and beyond.

values.title: Excellence
values.description: We strive for the highest standards in research, education, and innovation, ensuring our work meets international quality benchmarks.

values.title: Collaboration
values.description: We believe in the power of partnerships and work closely with institutions, researchers, and communities to achieve shared goals.

values.title: Innovation
values.description: We embrace creativity and cutting-edge approaches to solve complex problems and advance the frontiers of knowledge.

values.title: Capacity Building
values.description: We are committed to developing the next generation of African scientists and empowering communities through knowledge transfer.

values.title: Impact
values.description: We focus on research and training that makes a tangible difference in addressing Africa's most pressing challenges.

servicesSection.sectionDescription: We provide a comprehensive range of services to support computational research and capacity building across Africa.

services.title: Graduate Education
services.description: We offer MSc and PhD programs in Bioinformatics and Data-Intensive Sciences, providing rigorous training and research opportunities for the next generation of computational scientists.

services.title: Research Support
services.description: We facilitate cutting-edge research in bioinformatics, genomics, data science, and related fields through state-of-the-art infrastructure and expert guidance.

services.title: Training & Workshops
services.description: We conduct regular training programs, workshops, and short courses to build capacity in computational methods and data analysis across Africa.

services.title: High-Performance Computing
services.description: Our HPC cluster provides researchers with the computational power needed for data-intensive analyses and simulations.

services.title: Collaboration & Networking
services.description: We facilitate partnerships between researchers, institutions, and industry partners to advance computational sciences in Africa.

services.title: Data Science Consulting
services.description: We offer expert consulting services in data analysis, bioinformatics, and computational methods for research projects and organizations.

educationSection.description: Our education and training programs are designed to equip students and researchers with the skills needed to excel in computational sciences. We offer degree programs, short courses, and workshops that combine theoretical knowledge with hands-on practical experience. Our graduates go on to make significant contributions to research, industry, and policy-making across Africa and globally.

programs.title: MSc in Bioinformatics
programs.description: A comprehensive two-year program covering computational biology, genomics, and data analysis

programs.title: PhD in Data-Intensive Sciences
programs.description: Advanced research training in data science, bioinformatics, and computational methods

programs.title: Professional Development Courses
programs.description: Short courses and workshops in specialized topics for working professionals

milestones.title: Center Established
milestones.description: The African Center of Excellence in Bioinformatics and Data-Intensive Sciences was officially established at Makerere University with support from the World Bank.

milestones.title: First Graduate Cohort
milestones.description: We admitted our first cohort of MSc and PhD students, marking the beginning of our graduate education programs.

milestones.title: HPC Cluster Launch
milestones.description: We launched our state-of-the-art High-Performance Computing cluster, providing researchers with powerful computational resources.

milestones.title: International Partnerships
milestones.description: We established formal partnerships with leading universities in Europe and North America, expanding our collaborative research network.

milestones.title: Research Excellence
milestones.description: Our center surpassed 200 peer-reviewed publications and trained over 150 graduate students, cementing our position as a regional leader in computational sciences.

partnersSection.description: We work with leading institutions worldwide to advance computational research and training in Africa. Our partnerships enable us to provide world-class education, conduct cutting-edge research, and build sustainable capacity.

partnerTypes.description: Leading universities and research institutions worldwide

partners.name: University of Cambridge
partners.description: Collaborative research and student exchange programs

partners.name: Uppsala University
partners.description: Joint research projects and training programs

partners.name: H3Africa Consortium
partners.description: Human heredity and health research network

partnerTypes.description: Organizations supporting our mission and programs

partners.name: NIH Fogarty International Center
partners.description: Support for training and research programs
